lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, hello there, [player name]. Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Elias. So what brings you all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, hello there, [player name]. Good to see you. I take it you recived my message?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did indeed. So what brings us all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, it turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going so far?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Pretty well. In fact, I think I'm getting close to something. It's related to that old mastaba up on the plateau. I reckon it might contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: In fact, as you might recall, I was here before to look into that old mastaba up on the plateau.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I remember. You said it might contain important information on the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed I did. And I reckon it might also contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: And on that note, how do you feel about a bit of tomb raiding?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a job for me! What do you need?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, without getting bogged down in all the details, my research led me to some information on a rare and unusual form of necromancy that was once practiced in these lands.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The key element of the process involved the removal and preservation of the heart, much in the same way that the priests of Icthlarin do so during their burial ceremonies.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The key difference: The process is performed while the victim is still alive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's horrible!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed. I have encountered many forms of dark magic in my time, but I must confess, few have filled me with the same anger that I felt upon discovering this vile form of necromancy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think this is what happened to Arrav?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Almost certainly. The end result of the process is an undead slave, bound to whoever possess their still-beating heart.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The victim must do whatever they are commanded, but unlike most other forms of necromancy, their mind, and indeed their very soul, remains intact. It all perfectly matches what you encountered with Arrav.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now thankfully, as horrible as all this is, I believe this is a way for us to break Zemouregal's control over Arrav. To do so, we must recover Arrav's heart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what does that have to do with the mastaba?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: If we are to recover the heart, we will need a way to not only keep it preserved, but to keep it beating as well.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Zemouregal must have a way of doing this, but I have a feeling he won't be willing to share the details on how.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: My hope is the mastaba contains the answers we need. It was built to house a Pharaoh who ruled the desert thousands of years ago.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: She was known for many feats, with one of them being her work in saving the victims of a Mahjarrat necromancer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see. Well, if there's any information on heart preservation in there, I'm sure I can find it.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I have no doubt you can. While you work on that, I will get started on our next issue: Finding where Zemouregal is keeping Arrav's heart. Hopefully I'll have something by the time you're done.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Oh, and a word of warning. When I was asking around about the mastaba, I was told about a group of... well they apparently called themselves explorers. They entered the mastaba, but never came back out.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I'd say it's safe to assume that there are some sort of defensive measures in place to deter any would-be grave robbers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the heads up. I'll be careful.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Good luck in there. I will meet you here once you are done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll have to pass right now. I have some other things I need to do.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, when you're ready, you know where to find me.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, hello again, [player name]. How goes the tomb raiding?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You need to head into that mastaba up on the plateau and see if you can find some information on how we can keep Arrav's heart preserved. Once you have something, meet me back here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, keep at it. Once you have some information on how we can keep Arrav's heart preserved, meet me back here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've discovered what we need. We should be able to preserve Arrav's heart using a specially prepared canopic jar.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Excellent stuff! So how do we prepare this jar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to fill it with three dwellberries and a ring of life. I'll get started right away.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Sounds good. Let me know once it's ready and we can discuss our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to fill it with three dwellberries and a ring of life. I'm working on it right now.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, let me know once it's ready and we can discuss our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

sections.title: Searching the skeleton by the dais
Searching the skeleton by the entrance of the room with the boat
Searching the skeleton by the boat
Searching the south skeleton in the tunnel
Searching the north skeleton in the tunnel
Searching the skeleton south of the boat
Opening the imposing stone doors without pulling the levers
Pulling the lever after the floor grid
Using dwellberries on the canopic jar
Using a ring of life on the canopic jar

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, hello again, [player name]. How goes the tomb raiding?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This canopic jar should be able to preserve Arrav's heart!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Excellent stuff! I had no doubt you'd discover what we needed. You'll be pleased to hear that I've managed to make some good progress as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found where Arrav's heart is?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Not quite, but I have the next best thing. One of my contacts has been supporting my research by investigating Mahjarrat activity in the far north.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: He recently spotted Zemouregal entering an old fort near the mountain of Trollweiss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I know Trollweiss. I've been tobogganing there.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Really? It hadn't struck me as a tourist area, what with it being a bit troll-ridden. Anyhow, you shouldn't need to be doing any tobogganing this time. Instead, you'll be heading underground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Underground?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Yes. My contact located some tunnels that looked like they might lead right into the fort. Seems like a far better option than the front door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, then I'd best get going. Where can I find these tunnels?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The entrance is right on the top of Trollweiss. I'd suggest you take a pickaxe with you. My contact mentioned that there had been a few cave-ins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem to have a lot of contacts.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed. I've managed to make a few friends in my time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are they all looking into Zemouregal for you?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Oh, not at all. Given recent events, Zemouregal is of particular interest of course, but Mahjarrat activity in general has been increasing recently, especially up north.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you first end up researching the Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You know, it's been so long that I can barely remember. As I recall, I first found mention of their kind right here in the desert.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I was assisting some archologists in the south when I found an old tablet describing of a race of seemingly immortal shapeshifters wielding the power of gods. Naturally, I was immediately fascinated!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Of course, I soon went on to find out that they are not only fascinating, but also very dangerous. I must admit that I see it as something of a duty to keep my research up to date.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The events at Varrock showed that too many have forgotten the dangers of the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Good luck out there.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Have you managed to explore those tunnels yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You'll find the entrance atop Trollweiss Mountain. I'd suggest you take a pickaxe with you. My contact mentioned that there had been a few cave-ins.

sections.title: Talking to Arrav in the fort

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: You...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrav?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: You... You should not be here. Zemouregal could return at... urgh... at any time. If he does, I won't.... I won't be able to stop myself from... arrgh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a risk I'm willing to take. I'm here to help you.

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: Why? You do not... do not know me. You do not know what I've... urgh... what I've done. Leave this place, and leave me. I am... forsaken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not if I have anything to say about it. Arrav, I need to find your heart. With it, I can free you!

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: It's not... here. Zemouregal did not want it too close to his... arrgh... his kin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where it is?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: In the next room... look behind the... urgh... the flame tapestry. You might be able to find something that can... arrgh... help.

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: My master calls me... Thank you... whoever you are. I... urgh... I don't even know your name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's [player name], and I will save you Arrav, I promise.

sections.title: Attempting to open the south or west doors in the base

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. Did you manage to find a way into that fort?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did! The heart wasn't there, but I think I know where it is. I reckon it's in the base near Varrock.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, take a look at these plans I found and see what you think.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Right then, let's see...

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Yes, there's a chamber here marked as containing a heart! It must be Arrav's!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: However, reaching it is going to pose some significant problems...

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It seems that the inner section of Zemouregal's Base has state of the art security. The corridor into the area is filled to the brim with human detection spells. These will set off an alarm.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, there is a sewage system running under some parts of the base that can be accessed from the nearby kitchen.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: These sewers could be used to bypass the spells. However, surges of energy are periodically sent through the water there.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: On the other side of the spells the vault door is found. This door is guarded by zombies. If they see an intruder, they will set off the alarm.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: If the alarm is sounded, a gas will be released, incapacitating all living things in the area.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The vault door is made of five-inch thick runite-enforced steel, with a four digit access code. When the code is entered incorrectly more than twice, the alarm will be set off.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Inside the vault there is a criss-cross of special fire ray enchantments that Zemouregal has made. Touching any of these will do significant damage and set off the alarm.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Finally, at the back of the vault, we have the pedestal holding Arrav's heart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that sounds an awful lot to get past. It sounds like an impossible mission!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It does appear to pose somewhat of a challenge, but if we engage in a bit of cunning, it may not be as impossible as you might think.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: First of all, we're going to need a distraction to lure some of those zombies out. I have no doubt that I'll be able to act as suitable bait. While I do that, you'll need to get through those sewers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what about the energy surges? I don't really fancy getting fried!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Understandable. I would suggest you invest in some kind of insulation to protect yourself from the surges.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, provided my distraction works, you should find the area on the other side of the sewers free of zombies. That will give you the time and space needed to get that vault door open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then the fire rays?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well, no great heist is complete without the use of a crossbow and a grappling hook.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so to summarise, I need some sort of insulated protection along with a crossbow and grappling hook?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Precisely! Gather those items and then meet me outside Zemouregal's Base near Varrock. It's time to recover a heart!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. I found this earlier. I imagine you'll be needing it.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, are you ready to perform a heist? Remember, you'll need some sort of insulated protection along with a crossbow and grappling hook.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, there you are, [player name]. Ready to perform a heist? Remember, you'll need some sort of insulated protection along with a crossbow and grappling hook.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready when you are.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Excellent. Once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still need to do some preparation.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Very well. I will wait for you here.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. I found this earlier. I imagine you'll be needing it.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I'm ready when you are, [player name]. Once you head in, I'll arrange a distraction to lure some of those zombies away. I'll meet you back here once you've recovered the heart. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Well, look what we have here...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Did you really think you had any hope of stealing that heart?

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Arrav, kill [him/her/them].

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: Urgh... [player name]...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: What a miserable failure you are. Sharathteerk, get rid of him.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: What are you playing at?

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: Something's not right... Something's blocking my teleport!

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Impossible! Stop messing around and...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Wait... I sense...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: You!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: [player name]! Grab the heart, quickly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a close one!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: That's one way to put it. Good job I was keeping an eye on things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again, though that does leave me with some questions...

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You're wondering how I managed to do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Teleporting directly into the room... Keeping a Mahjarrat at bay... Those aren't the kind of skills that just anyone possesses. Not to mention that Zemouregal knew you!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I have never kept it secret from you that I have a history with the Mahjarrat, although I may have chosen not to divulge some of the more... specific details.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I hope you will not judge me too harshly for that. We are all entitled to our secrets, and even now I would prefer not to share them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess... It's just, you made out you were just researching the Mahjarrat. This is clearly a lot more than that.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It is, and I apologise if you feel I have deceived you. My past with the Mahjarrat, and especially with Zemouregal is... complicated. Again, I would prefer we not discuss this further.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, why don't we instead focus this conversation on something far more positive than my history. We have successfully recovered Arrav's heart!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So does this mean we can free him now?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Eventually, yes, but while the heart is key to Zemouregal's control over Arrav, merely possessing it won't be enough to break that control.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Doing so will require further research, not to mention the right opportunity.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I will take the heart and begin working right away. Given that Zemouregal knows we have it, I intend to go to ground for this one.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You might not hear from me for some time, but fear not, I will be in touch again once things are ready.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Stay safe out there, [player name]. Until we meet again.
